votes.reason: The Python implementation correctly computes the fib4 sequence iteratively, matching the recursive definition in Coq for all concrete inputs.
The Python implementation correctly implements the fib4 recurrence relation and base cases as defined in the Coq specification.
The Python code correctly implements the iterative calculation of the fib4 sequence defined in Coq, handling base cases and the recurrence relation identically.

The Python code implements the same fib4 sequence as the Coq code. Both define fib4(0)=0, fib4(1)=0, fib4(2)=2, fib4(3)=0, and for n>=4, fib4(n) = fib4(n-1) + fib4(n-2) + fib4(n-3) + fib4(n-4). The Python uses an iterative approach with four variables that correctly tracks the sliding window of the last four values, producing the same results as the Coq recursive definition for all non-negative integer inputs.

The Python version uses an iterative approach with four variables that correctly track the last four values, producing the same results as the Coq recursive definition for all non-negative integer inputs.

Python fib4 matches Coq fib4 base cases and recurrence for all n >= 0, and spec/pre mirror Coq’s (True pre, output equals fib4(input)).
Python iteratively implements the same base cases and four-term recurrence as the Coq fib4 for all n >= 0; precondition/spec match (True and output == fib4(input)).
Python matches Coq base cases and the 4-term recurrence for all n >= 0, using an iterative approach. Pre and spec mirror Coq’s (spec is output == fib4(input)).
